request_mainline: runtime asks the tool owner for a per-run registry
registry exports provider-neutral tool definitions without importing provider adapter DTOs
registry keeps Reasonix-aligned tool names, schemas, and `read_only` metadata in one owner
path-based read-only tools resolve against one locked workspace root: the canonical process current working directory
path-based tools resolve against one locked workspace root: the canonical process current working directory
runtime may choose a subset of implemented definitions for live execution
provider adapters render schemas; they do not own tool registry truth
response_mainline: first real read-only execution set is: `read_file`, `glob`, `grep`, `ls`
first real file-mutation execution set is: `write_file`, `edit_file`, `multi_edit`
implemented tools return user/model-visible tool result text
unsupported or unimplemented tools fail explicitly and do not become successful tool-result truth
error_mainline: registered but not implemented tools return `ToolRegistryError::UnimplementedTool`

shared_functions.purpose: derive the canonical locked workspace root for all first-version path tools
shared_functions.why_shared: keeps directory-lock truth in one owner helper instead of per-tool duplication

shared_functions.purpose: resolve path arguments and reject escapes outside the locked workspace root
resolve writable path targets inside the locked workspace root even when the target file does not yet exist
persist file-mutation tool output through one owner-controlled temp-file-and-rename path
shared_functions.why_shared: keeps mutation write semantics centralized instead of per-tool duplication

shared_functions.purpose: enforce exact single-match replacement semantics for text mutation tools
render structured tool arguments without duplicating JSON conversion in runtime/tool owner code
shared_functions.why_shared: keeps tool argument conversion as a shared block instead of per-crate helpers

call_table.responsibility: create per-run built-in registry aligned with Reasonix names and schemas
declare built-in tool metadata, schema, read-only state, and implementation state
export currently executable provider-neutral tool schemas
dispatch completed tool calls into the single owner implementation set
read UTF-8 text from one locked in-root file with line-windowing
call_table.input_semantic: path plus optional offset plus optional limit

call_table.responsibility: create or overwrite one UTF-8 text file inside the locked root
replace one exact text occurrence in one locked in-root file
apply ordered exact text edits and write once at the end
match in-root files by glob pattern with recursive filename fallback
search in-root UTF-8 text files by regex
list directory entries or recursive tree under locked root

sync_status: Reasonix-aligned built-in names and schemas are bound in `freehand-tools`
first-version path tools are locked to the canonical process current working directory and reject path escape outside that root
first-version file-mutation tools are text-only, workspace-locked, require existing parent directories, and write through one atomic owner path
bash/web/notebook/symbol-aware mutation tools remain registered but explicitly unimplemented until their lifecycle/gates are designed
